cmd:
set -e
BIN=/var/lib/easyenclave/bin
SRC=$BIN/podman-linux-amd64
until [ -x $SRC/usr/local/bin/podman ]; do sleep 1; done
# Wait for mount-models to mount /dev/vdc before writing under
# /var/lib/easyenclave/ollama — otherwise writes land on tmpfs
# and get shadowed the moment vdc is mounted. On VMs without vdc
# (e.g. CP previews with no models disk) this check short-circuits.
if [ -b /dev/vdc ]; then
  until mountpoint -q /var/lib/easyenclave/ollama 2>/dev/null; do sleep 1; done
fi
mkdir -p /var/lib/easyenclave/ollama
# Stage helpers first (conmon, netavark, crun, etc.).
for f in $SRC/usr/local/bin/*; do
  name=$(basename $f)
  case $name in
    podman) cp -f $f $BIN/.podman-raw ;;
    *)      cp -f $f $BIN/ ;;
  esac
done
cp -f $SRC/usr/local/lib/podman/conmon $BIN/
cp -f $SRC/usr/local/lib/podman/netavark $BIN/ 2>/dev/null || true
cp -f $SRC/usr/local/lib/podman/aardvark-dns $BIN/ 2>/dev/null || true
cp -f $SRC/usr/local/lib/podman/rootlessport $BIN/ 2>/dev/null || true
mkdir -p /var/lib/easyenclave/ollama/.podman/storage /var/lib/easyenclave/ollama/.podman/runroot
# /dev/shm holds podman's per-container POSIX shm lock file
# (libpod_lock). EE may not mount tmpfs there; without it, podman
# fails `failed to create 2048 locks in /libpod_lock`. Idempotent.
if ! mountpoint -q /dev/shm 2>/dev/null; then
  mkdir -p /dev/shm
  mount -t tmpfs -o size=64M tmpfs /dev/shm 2>/dev/null || true
fi
# Pick storage driver: overlay on vdc-backed ext4; vfs elsewhere
# (overlay-on-tmpfs errors out).
if mountpoint -q /var/lib/easyenclave/ollama; then
  DRIVER=overlay
else
  DRIVER=vfs
fi
POL=/var/lib/easyenclave/ollama/.podman/policy.json
printf '%s' '{"default":[{"type":"insecureAcceptAnything"}]}' > $POL
# /etc and /root are RO on EE. Build a writable fake HOME for
# policy.json + podman's default lookups.
HOME_DIR=/var/lib/easyenclave/.home
mkdir -p $HOME_DIR/.config/containers $HOME_DIR/tmp
cp -f $POL $HOME_DIR/.config/containers/policy.json
CONF=/var/lib/easyenclave/ollama/.podman/containers.conf
printf '%s\n' '[engine]' 'helper_binaries_dir = ["/var/lib/easyenclave/bin"]' > $CONF
# Wrapper installed as $BIN/podman so bare `podman ps` (from PATH)
# reaches the right storage root + driver. Raw binary lives at
# $BIN/.podman-raw. $BIN/dd-podman stays as a back-compat symlink
# since openclaw's workload calls dd-podman by name.
printf '%s\n' '#!/bin/sh' "export HOME=$HOME_DIR" "export TMPDIR=$HOME_DIR/tmp" "export CONTAINERS_CONF=$CONF" "exec $BIN/.podman-raw --conmon=$BIN/conmon --runtime=$BIN/crun --storage-driver=$DRIVER --root=/var/lib/easyenclave/ollama/.podman/storage --runroot=/var/lib/easyenclave/ollama/.podman/runroot --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs \"\$@\"" > $BIN/podman
chmod +x $BIN/podman
ln -sf podman $BIN/dd-podman
ls -la $CONF $POL $BIN/podman $BIN/dd-podman $BIN/.podman-raw 2>&1 || true
echo podman-bootstrap: ok driver=$DRIVER conf=$CONF policy=$POL
